# What Is a Bankruptcy Mill?
> Last updated: 2026-05-01

> How to spot a bankruptcy mill before you hire one. Warning signs, exposed practices, and data-driven red flags. Protect your case - free 2026 guide.

## About
bankruptcymill.org is part of the Open Bankruptcy Project (openbankruptcyproject.org), a 501(c)(3) nonprofit building free, open-source tools for bankruptcy court transparency.

## Key Pages
- Bankruptcy Mill Outcomes Data: What Federal Courts Show: https://bankruptcymill.org/data-on-outcomes.html
- How to Find a Bankruptcy Lawyer - Bankruptcy Mill: https://bankruptcymill.org/find-a-lawyer.html
- Bankruptcy Mill FAQ - How to Spot and Avoid High-Volume Firms: https://bankruptcymill.org/frequently-asked-questions.html
- How to Spot a Bankruptcy Mill: Warning Signs: https://bankruptcymill.org/how-to-spot.html
- What Is a Bankruptcy Mill?: https://bankruptcymill.org/
- 10 Questions to Ask a Bankruptcy Attorney Before Hiring - Bankruptcy Mill: https://bankruptcymill.org/questions-to-ask.html
- Red Flags Your Bankruptcy Attorney May Be a Mill - 15 Warning Signs - Bankruptcy Mill: https://bankruptcymill.org/red-flags.html
- 10 Warning Signs You Hired a Bankruptcy Mill: https://bankruptcymill.org/warning-signs.html
- What To Do If You Think You Hired a Bankruptcy Mill: https://bankruptcymill.org/what-to-do.html
- Why Do Chapter 13 Cases Fail? - Bankruptcy Mill: https://bankruptcymill.org/why-cases-fail.html
- Privacy Policy: https://bankruptcymill.org/privacy.html

## Contact
- Website: https://openbankruptcyproject.org
- Email: contact@openbankruptcyproject.org
- GitHub: https://github.com/openbankruptcyproject

## License
Content is provided for educational purposes. Data sourced from the Federal Judicial Center Integrated Database (4.9M cases, 94 districts).
